theory.intro: If you cut a shape into pieces and rearrange them into a different shape, the total area stays the same. This idea turns hard shapes into easy ones.
facts: Cutting and rearranging **preserves area** — no surface is added or lost.
A parallelogram can be cut and rearranged into a **rectangle** with the same base and height.
Two equal triangles can be joined to make a parallelogram or rectangle.
steps: Cut the shape into pieces (often along a straight line).
Move the pieces to form a simpler shape, such as a rectangle.
The area is unchanged, so find the area of the simpler shape.

foundation.q: A shape of area $20\text{ cm}^{2}$ is cut and rearranged into a rectangle. What is the rectangle's area?
A parallelogram is cut along its height and rearranged into a rectangle $6$ cm by $4$ cm. Find the parallelogram's area.
Two identical triangles are joined to form a rectangle of area $30\text{ cm}^{2}$. Find the area of one triangle.
A square of area $16\text{ cm}^{2}$ is cut into two rectangles. What is the total area of the two pieces?
A rectangle is cut along a diagonal into two triangles. If the rectangle is $8$ cm by $3$ cm, find the area of one triangle.
A shape of area $35\text{ m}^{2}$ is rearranged into a different shape. What is the new area?
A parallelogram becomes a rectangle $10$ cm by $5$ cm when cut and rearranged. Find its area.
Two equal triangles each of area $9\text{ cm}^{2}$ are joined into a parallelogram. Find the parallelogram's area.
An L-shape of area $24\text{ cm}^{2}$ is rearranged into a rectangle. What is the rectangle's area?
A rectangle $6$ cm by $6$ cm is cut into four equal squares. Find the area of one square.

development.q: A parallelogram with base $12$ cm and height $7$ cm is rearranged into a rectangle. Find the rectangle's area and a possible pair of dimensions.
A triangle of area $40\text{ cm}^{2}$ is paired with an identical triangle to make a rectangle. Give the rectangle's area.
An L-shape ($6$ cm by $4$ cm rectangle plus $2$ cm by $3$ cm rectangle) is rearranged into a single rectangle of width $3$ cm. Find its length.
A parallelogram is cut and rearranged into a $15$ cm by $8$ cm rectangle. Find its area.
A square of area $36\text{ cm}^{2}$ is rearranged into a rectangle of length $9$ cm. Find the rectangle's width.
Four right-angled triangles, each of area $6\text{ cm}^{2}$, are arranged to make a square. Find the square's area.
A trapezium is cut into a rectangle ($8$ cm by $3$ cm) and a triangle (area $6\text{ cm}^{2}$). Find the trapezium's area.
A parallelogram of base $9$ m and height $6$ m is rearranged into a rectangle of length $9$ m. Find its width.
An L-shape of area $40\text{ cm}^{2}$ is rearranged into a rectangle $8$ cm long. Find its width.
A rectangle $12$ cm by $5$ cm is cut along a diagonal and the two triangles rearranged into a parallelogram. Find the parallelogram's area.

mastery.q: A parallelogram of base $14$ cm and height $9$ cm is rearranged into a rectangle. If the rectangle is $7$ cm wide, find its length.
An L-shape is rearranged into a square. If the L-shape has area $49\text{ cm}^{2}$, find the side of the square.
Two identical triangles join to form a parallelogram of area $96\text{ cm}^{2}$, base $12$ cm. Find the height and the area of one triangle.
A shape is dissected into a $5$ cm by $4$ cm rectangle and two triangles each $5\text{ cm}^{2}$, then rearranged. Find the total area.
A $10$ cm by $6$ cm rectangle is cut into pieces and rearranged into a parallelogram of base $12$ cm. Find the parallelogram's height.
A tangram-style square of area $64\text{ cm}^{2}$ is rearranged into a rectangle of width $8$ cm. Explain why its length must be $8$ cm.
mastery.a: $8$ cm, because area and width fix the length

mastery.q: A parallelogram and a rectangle are made from the same pieces. The rectangle is $9$ cm by $6$ cm. Without recomputing pieces, give the parallelogram's area.
An L-shaped paddock of area $90\text{ m}^{2}$ is to be re-fenced as a single rectangle of the same area, $9$ m wide. Find its length.
A trapezium is split into a rectangle and two triangles, total area $48\text{ cm}^{2}$, then rearranged into a rectangle $6$ cm wide. Find its length.
A shape has area $72\text{ cm}^{2}$. It is rearranged into a rectangle with whole-number sides, one of which is $9$ cm. Find the other side.
